commentary_text: The model favors Japan to win by 17 percentage points over the sportsbook consensus, while both agree Japan is the favorite. The market assigns Sweden nearly triple the win probability the model does—21.5% versus 7.6%—suggesting bookmakers are pricing in information the statistical model cannot see, such as recent form shifts, lineup changes, or injury concerns affecting one or both teams. The dashboard surfaces this substantial divergence as a signal worth monitoring rather than a judgment on which forecast is more reliable.
